content.claim: Church-run schools in Hungary have doubled since 2010, enjoy student-selection freedom and greater autonomy, whereas state-run schools must accept all catchment students; this potentially concentrates disadvantage in remaining state village schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Does not quantify actual enrollment diversion from state rural schools to church schools
Does not establish counterfactual: what state village school composition would be without church-school option
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
